items.q: Are public toilets free in Berlin?
items.a: Most are not. A standard Berliner Toilette costs 50 cents per use. As of 2026, 107 of the 278 automatic cabins are being made permanently free and gender-neutral after a successful pilot, but the remaining 171 still charge 50 cents. Train station toilets usually cost around 1 euro.

items.q: How do I pay for a Berlin public toilet?
items.a: Berliner Toiletten accept coins, contactless bank card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and direct payment through the official Berliner Toilette app. Carry at least one 50 cent coin as a fallback for any street cabin.

items.q: Where can I find a clean public toilet near tourist sights in Berlin?
items.a: The easiest comfortable options near the main central route are department stores and malls: Galeria on Alexanderplatz, Mall of Berlin near Potsdamer Platz, Galeries Lafayette near Friedrichstrasse, and KaDeWe in the West. Major train stations also have larger staffed toilet centers.

items.q: Is there an app for public toilets in Berlin?
items.a: Yes. The official Berliner Toilette app shows the nearest cabins on a live map, indicates which are in service, and lets you pay through the app instead of with a coin. It is available on iOS and Android.

items.q: Are Berlin's public toilets wheelchair accessible?
items.a: Yes. The automatic Berliner Toiletten cabins are barrier-free and include grab rails inside. People with a EuroKey, the European disability key, can open the cabins for free. Train station toilet centers also have accessible cubicles.
